response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with broad web, mobile, and embedded experience, but the role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear in the resume. There is no evidence of GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, performance analysis at the hardware level, or PhD/Master's-level specialization typical for a Principal Performance Architect role.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background across many stacks and domains
Some low-level C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and embedded systems experience
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI accelerator experience
No deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Educational/experience profile does not match Principal hardware-architecture seniority requirements

key_factors.factor: Missing hardware architecture and SoC modeling expertise
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload optimization experience
Limited low-level C/C++ and embedded exposure
